title: North American Clean Fuels Market: Month-in-Review April 2025
content:
Key Takeaways:

California LCFS:
  • CA LCFS average Type 1 monthly credit price fell by 0.34% month-over-month (MoM) in April.
  • Monthly credit transactions rose by 61.28% MoM in April, reaching the highest trading volume ever recorded in the programâ€™s history.
British Columbia LCFS:
  • BC LCFS monthly trading volumes saw a rise of 4.99x times MoM.
  • In March, BC LCFS rose in credit prices by 42.13% MoM.
Oregon CFP:
  • In March, OR CFP average credit prices decreased by 13.26% compared to February.
  • OR CFP trading activity in March saw a 1.89x times rise MoM.
Canada CFR:
  • Canada CFR bid and offer prices for CC2 rose MoM in April.
  • CC3 offer prices also increased MoM and bid prices were reported at CAD100.
Washington CFS:
  • Average credit prices in WA CFS rose by 1.76% MoM.
  • For March, 236.09K credits were traded with a total transaction value of $5.33M.
Â United States RFS:
  • RIN generation increased by 8.56% in March.
  • A total of 6.73B RINs were transacted in March, 34.70% more than February.
author: Mansift Kaur
